solution.p: The first step is to subtract terms in order to separate the variable and non-variable terms. \begin{aligned} 6c +2 \amp = c +12 \\ 6c +2\mathbf{{}-c-2} \amp = c +12\mathbf{{}-c-2} \\ 5c \amp = 10 \\ \frac{5c}{5} \amp = \frac{10}{5} \\ c \amp = 2 \end{aligned}

The solution to this equation is 2. To stress that this is a value assigned to c, some report c = 2. We can also say that the solution set is \{2\}, or that c\in\left\{2\right\}. If we substitute 2 in for c in the original equation 6c+2=c+12, the equation will be true. Please check this on your own; it is an important habit.
